address: Sway Residences — Dubai Hills Estate, 1BR / 763 sqft

rent_snapshot.verdict: Rent & value note: The “from AED 965k” headline is for Sway's smallest (~450–600 sqft) units only. A genuine 763 sqft 1BR realistically sits at ~AED 1.40M–1.55M. Yields above are on the ~AED 1.48M fair value; if a 763 sqft unit is secured near AED 1.25M, gross rises to ~8.8% and net to ~6.3%. Occupancy is >94% community-wide with a deep professional/family tenant pool.

investment_metrics.cap_rate_status: Gross 7.4% at fair value; ~8.8% if bought near AED 1.25M
investment_metrics.coc_status: Positive leverage — cheap 4.4% debt vs ~6% net yield
investment_metrics.grm_status: Price-to-annual-rent at fair value; ~11.4x at AED 1.25M
investment_metrics.dscr_status: Slightly below 1.25 ideal at 80% LTV — use 60% LTV as investor base
investment_metrics.one_pct_status: Below 1% rule — normal for Dubai capital-growth markets
investment_metrics.breakeven_status: Buy-and-hold; flip needs ~11-13% appreciation to clear costs

details.notes: Kings' Dubai Hills, GEMS Wellington nearby

details.detail: Dubai Hills Mall (650 outlets), 180k sqm park, golf

details.notes: DIFC/Marina ~20-25 min; no metro until Blue Line

strategies.pros: Positive leverage, tax-free income + gains, resilient end-user community
strategies.risk: Supply wave; DSCR thin if rates rise above 5%
Capped upside on a ready unit

scenarios.trigger: Population surge, Blue Line momentum, DHE outperforms, supply absorbed
Maturing cycle: modest price growth, flat-to-soft apartment rents
120k city-wide handovers oversupply apartments, rents discount, EIBOR >5%

recommendation.summary: Sway Residences offers value entry into a Grade-A community (neighborhood 86/100) at a ~10-25% per-sqft discount to adjacent Emaar towers. Income is strong and resilient — ~7.4% gross / ~5.3% net at fair value, rising to ~8.8%/6.3% if secured near AED 1.25M, with >94% occupancy and positive leveraged cash flow. As an end-user-driven community, Dubai Hills offers liquidity, price stability, and a dual buyer pool on exit. Main watch-items: a maturing market (QoQ price growth ~0%) and a heavy 2026 city-wide apartment supply wave — so buy on yield, hold 5+ years, and treat this as buy-and-hold rather than a flip.
recommendation.suggested_offer: Target ≤ AED 1,450,000 (ideal AED 1.25-1.35M if a true 763 sqft unit is available)
action_items: Verify the exact size/price pairing — confirm 763 sqft against the live price list (the 965k headline is for smaller units)
Pull DLD sold comps for Sway 1BR to confirm fair value before offering
Confirm the current service charge (AED/sqft) and reserve-fund health
Model financing at 60% LTV (investor reality) and stress-test at +1% EIBOR
Hold 5+ years; do not underwrite this as a short-term flip

risk_factors.notes: ~120k city-wide handovers + ~5,600 DHE pipeline pressures the apartment segment
‘From 965k’ is for ~450-600 sqft units; verify the 763 sqft price and DLD records
DHE charges trending up; each +AED 2/sqft cuts ~AED 1,526/yr from NOI
DSCR ~1.19 at 80% LTV; thin if EIBOR rises above 5%
QoQ price growth ~0%; ~1% Dubai-wide forecast 2026 — capital growth modest near-term
